channel.title: Applied Linguistics Review
channel.description: A RSS feed containing the latest articles published in Applied Linguistics Review

item.title: Frontmatter
item.description: Article by Sofia Davydenko, Alastair Henry DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/applirev-2025-frontmatter1

item.title: Disciplinary tribes and the discourse of mainstream media expert opinion articles: evidencing COVID-19 knowledge claims for a public audience
item.description: Article by Sofia Davydenko, Alastair Henry DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/applirev-2023-0260

item.title: Ways of seeing and discourse strategies of naming the novel coronavirus in the US and Hong Kong
item.description: Article by Sofia Davydenko, Alastair Henry DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/applirev-2023-0266

item.title: Who is our friend and who is our enemy? The enregisterment of tribalising digital discourse during the COVID-19 pandemic
item.description: Article by Sofia Davydenko, Alastair Henry DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/applirev-2023-0269

item.title: “By the way I want to give you some masks”: exploring multimodal stance-taking in YouTube videos
item.description: Article by Sofia Davydenko, Alastair Henry DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/applirev-2023-0262

item.title: Affective geographies and tribal epistemologies: studying abroad during COVID-19
item.description: Article by Sofia Davydenko, Alastair Henry DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/applirev-2023-0254

item.title: Tribal epistemologies and the discursive construction of COVID-19 knowledge
item.description: Article by Sofia Davydenko, Alastair Henry DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/applirev-2023-0265

item.title: The myopic focus on decoloniality in applied linguistics and English language education: citations and stolen subjectivities
item.description: Article by Sofia Davydenko, Alastair Henry DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/applirev-2024-0011

item.title: The bidirectionality of epistemological theft and appropriation: contrastive rhetoric in China
item.description: Article by Sofia Davydenko, Alastair Henry DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/applirev-2024-0019

item.title: Attempts at including, mediating and creating ‘new’ knowledges: problematising appropriation in intercultural communication education and research
item.description: Article by Sofia Davydenko, Alastair Henry DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/applirev-2024-0009

item.title: Epistemological theft and appropriation in qualitative inquiry in applied linguistics: lessons from Halaqa
item.description: Article by Sofia Davydenko, Alastair Henry DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/applirev-2024-0013

item.title: Can the subaltern speak in autoethnography?: knowledging through dialogic and retro/intro/pro-spective reflection to stand against epistemic violence
item.description: Article by Sofia Davydenko, Alastair Henry DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/applirev-2024-0010

item.title: The violence of literature review and the imperative to ask new questions
item.description: Article by Sofia Davydenko, Alastair Henry DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/applirev-2024-0014

item.title: Against epistemological theft and appropriation in applied linguistics research
item.description: Article by Sofia Davydenko, Alastair Henry DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/applirev-2024-0008

item.title: ‘Art as social practice: language and marginality’: Special Issue of Applied Linguistics Review
item.description: Article by Sofia Davydenko, Alastair Henry DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/applirev-2024-0056

item.title: Objects are not just a thing – (re)negotiating identity through using material objects within the Kurdish diaspora in the UK
item.description: Article by Sofia Davydenko, Alastair Henry DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/applirev-2024-0055

item.title: “I am surprised they have allowed you in here to do this”: women’s prison writing as heterotopic space of narrative inclusion
item.description: Article by Sofia Davydenko, Alastair Henry DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/applirev-2024-0058

item.title: Walking with: understandings and negotiations of the mundane in research
item.description: Article by Sofia Davydenko, Alastair Henry DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/applirev-2024-0069

item.title: Translanguaging art – Questioning boundaries in Monika Szydłowska’s Do you miss your country?
item.description: Article by Sofia Davydenko, Alastair Henry DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/applirev-2024-0062

item.title: Reinventing the self through participatory art: writing and performing among rough sleepers
item.description: Article by Sofia Davydenko, Alastair Henry DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/applirev-2024-0060

item.title: Expectation-practice discrepancies: a transcultural exploration of Chinese students’ oral discourse socialization in German academia
item.description: Article by Sofia Davydenko, Alastair Henry DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/applirev-2022-0137

item.title: Perceived teacher feedback practices, student feedback motivation and engagement in English learning: a survey of Chinese university students
item.description: Article by Sofia Davydenko, Alastair Henry DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/applirev-2023-0136

item.title: Incidental vocabulary learning from listening, reading, and viewing captioned videos: frequency and prior vocabulary knowledge
item.description: Article by Sofia Davydenko, Alastair Henry DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/applirev-2023-0106

item.title: A longitudinal study on lecture listening difficulties and self-regulated learning strategies across different proficiency levels in EMI higher education
item.description: Article by Sofia Davydenko, Alastair Henry DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/applirev-2023-0113

item.title: Secondary students’ L2 writing motivation and engagement: the impact of teachers’ instructional approaches and feedback practices
item.description: Article by Sofia Davydenko, Alastair Henry DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/applirev-2022-0200

item.title: Marked on the voice: the visibility experiences of Russian heritage migrants following the war against Ukraine
item.description: Article by Sofia Davydenko, Alastair Henry DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/applirev-2023-0042
